name: BNIA Vital Signs
organization: Baltimore Neighborhood Indicators Alliance — Jacob France Institute (BNIAJFI)
limitations: Some metrics are composite or model-based. Longitudinal coverage requires manual download per year (no single time-series API). Vital Signs 22 may lag 12–18 months on some sub-components. Not all 150+ indicators are available for every year.
notes: Primary CSA-level data spine. Variable naming convention uses two-digit year suffix (e.g., le22 = life expectancy 2022). Approximately 150 total indicators across health, housing, economy, education, and crime domains. CSA names must be reconciled with csa_boundaries.geojson.

name: City Health Dashboard
organization: NYU School of Medicine / City Health Dashboard
limitations: Census-tract data available only for some metrics. Year coverage is inconsistent across indicators; some fields may be 2–3 years behind. Does not use CSA geography — not suitable as a primary neighborhood source.
notes: Best used to populate city/state/national benchmark values in BENCHMARKS.city, BENCHMARKS.state, BENCHMARKS.federal. Baltimore City is available as a named 'place' in the API. No authentication required for public data reads.

name: CDC PLACES
organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
data_type: Model-based small-area estimates (BRFSS multilevel regression and poststratification)
limitations: Values are MODELED ESTIMATES — not direct clinical counts or survey microdata. Confidence intervals can be wide at tract level. Not directly comparable to BNIA asthma ED visit rate (different numerator and denominator). Must be labeled 'CDC PLACES modeled estimate' whenever displayed. Baltimore City FIPS: 2404000.
notes: Dataset ID cwsq-ngmh is census-tract level. Socrata app token recommended for production. CSA-level estimates require building a tract-to-CSA crosswalk and applying population-weighted aggregation. proxy_metrics metadata flag in data.json should be set when PLACES asthma is used in place of BNIA asthma ED.

name: Open Baltimore 311 Customer Service Requests
years_available: 2015–present (2015–2019 via legacy Socrata ID 9agw-sxsr; 2020–2026 via per-year ArcGIS Hub datasets)
limitations: No neighborhood/CSA field in raw data — all CSA attribution requires a point-in-polygon spatial join with csa_boundaries.geojson. Request categories are user-selected and inconsistently applied. Duplicate requests for the same incident are common. Resolution time is affected by staffing and city workflow changes, not only neighborhood conditions.
notes: Fields: request type, created date, closed date, status, lat/lng, ZIP code. Socrata SODA API supports $where, $limit, $offset parameters for filtered downloads. Per-year ArcGIS Hub URLs follow the pattern: https://data.baltimorecity.gov/datasets/baltimore::311-customer-service-requests-YYYY. Legacy ID 9agw-sxsr covers pre-2020 history.

name: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates
data_type: Survey estimates with margins of error
limitations: 5-year estimates lag approximately 18 months behind the reference period end date. MOEs can be large for small geographic units (tracts with population under 1,000). Not designed for year-over-year change analysis at fine geographies due to overlapping survey windows. Free API key required — register at https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html.
notes: Key table references: B17001 (poverty), B23025 (employment status), B19013 (median HH income), B15003 (educational attainment), B25070 (rent burden), B18101 (disability), B08201 (vehicle access), B28002 (internet access). Baltimore City FIPS as place: 2404000; as county: 24510. API key must be stored as environment variable, not committed to source code.

name: EPA EJScreen
limitations: EJScreen is a screening tool only — values must not be used as causal health exposure measurements or for regulatory determinations. Block-group estimates have wide uncertainty ranges; treat as relative ranking, not precise exposure. Data currency varies by underlying source; some components lag 2–3 years. Must be labeled 'EPA EJScreen screening estimate' wherever displayed. New in version 2.3: NO2 indicator added (July 2024).
notes: Download Maryland block-group CSV from the EJScreen download page (no API key needed). Aggregate to CSA using population-weighted mean after spatial join with csa_boundaries.geojson. Key columns: PM25, OZONE, DSLPM, PTRAF, LDPNT, PNPL, PWDIS, UST, DEMOGIDX, EJ_INDEXD2_B. Block group GEOID can be joined to census crosswalk files for spatial aggregation.

name: Baltimore Area Survey
geographic_levels: Baltimore City (surveyed); CSA requires pooling ≥2 waves
data_type: Probability sample survey (n ≈ 748 Baltimore City respondents per wave)
limitations: Single-wave sample (n ≈ 748) is insufficient for reliable CSA-level estimates in most CSAs — requires pooling ≥2 waves. Non-response and coverage biases typical of telephone/online surveys. Must be labeled 'Resident perception survey — Baltimore Area Survey, Johns Hopkins 21CC' and clearly distinguished from administrative or clinical data. Not all CSAs are equally represented in each wave.
notes: Pooled 2023+2024 waves yield n ≈ 1,496 for CSA-level aggregation. Data available as public CSV on GitHub. Topics include: safety perception (day/night), neighborhood quality satisfaction, crime concern, housing stability, and climate adaptation willingness. Waves are released each December. Label pooled year range whenever CSA estimates are displayed.

name: Bureau of Labor Statistics Public Data API
data_type: Administrative labor force statistics (CPS + LAUS program)
limitations: No sub-city or neighborhood-level data. Annual LAUS estimates at small-area level carry modeling uncertainty. Largely superseded for neighborhood estimates by BNIA (which draws from ACS). Anonymous access limited to 25 daily queries — API key strongly recommended for production use.
notes: Key series IDs: LAUCN245100000000003A (Baltimore City annual unemployment rate), LASST240000000000003 (Maryland), LNS14000000 (U.S. national). API v2 accepts POST requests with JSON body containing 'seriesid' array and 'startyear'/'endyear' fields. Free API key registration at https://data.bls.gov/registrationEngine/.

name: FRED — Federal Reserve Economic Data
organization: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis
years_available: Varies by series; most unemployment series from 1990s onward
data_type: Administrative/derived (re-packages BLS, Census, and BEA data)
limitations: API key required (unlike BLS public API). Largely duplicates BLS unemployment data. No sub-city geography. Income series for small geographies are better sourced from ACS. Adds dependency without meaningfully extending coverage.
notes: Key series IDs: MDBALT5URN (Baltimore City monthly unemployment rate), MDUR (Maryland), UNRATE (U.S. national). Free API key at https://fred.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/api_key.html. Implement only if BLS API proves difficult to integrate or if additional macroeconomic series (e.g., housing price index) are added to a future dashboard tab.
